title: The Impact of AI Tools on Students' Cognitive Processes in Practical Software Development
study_design_description: An online questionnaire was used, combining closed and open-ended questions about students' background, use of AI tools, behavior during implementation, understanding and cognitive processes, motivation, and the teacher's role. Data were collected during lessons through a voluntary and anonymous online survey. Quantitative data were analyzed using descriptive statistics, while qualitative responses were examined through content analysis focusing on the link between AI use and cognitive engagement.
primary_task_description: Participants were high school students from grammar and vocational tracks with access to AI tools in programming classes.
population_description: Participants were high school students from grammar and vocational tracks with access to AI tools in programming classes.

outcomes.outcome_description: Use of ChatGPT as primary AI tool for programming
Students who do not use AI tools at all
Students who first attempt to solve the problem independently before asking AI for help
Students who at least try to understand the generated code before applying it
Students who simply copy and run the code without understanding
Students who feel that using AI bypasses their own reasoning
Students who disagree that using AI bypasses their own reasoning
Students who stated that obtaining a correct solution through AI motivates them to continue working
Students who believe that AI helps them progress faster
Students whose motivation decreases if they do not immediately obtain a correct solution
Students who agreed that the teacher is necessary to ensure that AI use genuinely contributes to learning
Students who did not reject the statement that using AI might bypass their own thought process

reported_statistics.verbatim_quote: The survey results (N=131) show that the vast majority of students use ChatGPT as their primary AI tool for programming (77.5%)
GitHub Copilot is used by only 12.5%
Interestingly, one fifth of students (20%) reported that they do not use AI tools at all.
Regarding solution implementation, a considerable share of students (38%) reported that they first attempt to solve the problem independently before asking AI for help
while 31% stated that they at least try to understand the generated code before applying it
Only a small portion (around 15%) admitted to simply copying and running the code.
However, perceptions of their own thinking process are more ambivalent: about 41% of students feel that using AI bypasses their own reasoning
while one third disagrees with this claim
The motivational effects of AI are evident: more than 70% of students stated that obtaining a correct solution through AI motivates them to continue working
and 68% believe that AI helps them progress faster
At the same time, nearly 30% of students admitted that their motivation decreases if they do not immediately obtain a correct solution
The teacher's role remains crucial: nearly 80% of students agreed that the teacher is necessary to ensure that AI use genuinely contributes to learning.
More than half of the students (54.96%) did not reject the statement that using AI might bypass their own thought process

notes: This is a survey-based observational study with no experimental control group or randomized assignment. The paper reports descriptive statistics from N=131 high school students regarding their use of AI tools (primarily ChatGPT) in programming education. No causal comparisons between AI-assisted and no-AI conditions are made. The study integrates survey data with theoretical frameworks (Cognitive Load Theory, Self-Determination Theory, Cognitive Flexibility Theory) but does not test causal effects of AI on learning outcomes. All reported statistics are descriptive percentages or proportions from the overall sample. The study identifies concerns about students' cognitive engagement, code understanding, and potential over-reliance on AI tools, but these are observational findings rather than experimental results.
